title: goodenia viridula
intro: Goodenia viridula, also known as the green goodenia or green mouse ear, is a plant species native to eastern Australia, characterized by its small, hairy leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Goodenia viridula is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
